key_insight: A configurable chemical validity operator converts heuristic oxidation-state rules into a tunable prior that screens and guides generative materials models toward more plausible inorganic compositions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical filter-and-reward layer for ultra-high-throughput materials discovery, improving the reliability of AI-generated candidates without fully sacrificing exploratory search.
method: Built on SMACT with a data-informed oxidation-state model and user-tunable thresholds
Evaluated six state-of-the-art inorganic crystal generative models against chemical plausibility and convex-hull proximity
Used the same operator both as a screening filter and as a reinforcement-learning reward for latent diffusion generation
evidence_signal: Benchmarking showed most models captured stoichiometry but often missed realistic oxidation-state combinations; filtering removed rare-oxidation-state compositions while retaining many low-energy compounds near the convex hull.
watch_item: Chemical rules are configurable rather than absolute, so overly conservative thresholds may exclude valid novel chemistries or bias exploration toward well-observed states.
paper_title: Chemical filters for ultra-high-throughput materials screening and generation
